text: Over the past decade, bicycloalkanes, including derivatives of cubane, have emerged as a new class of sp3-rich bioisosteres for (hetero)arenes to address the important challenge of enhancing the metabolism stability and solubility of lead compounds. Consequently, the synthesis of these complex 3D frameworks has been achieved through a multitude of different new methodologies. In this review we highlight recent advancements in photoredox mediated syntheses of bioisosteres that have been exploited for the preparation of bicycloalkanes (BCs) derivatives as well as a range of their structural analogues including; [1.1.1]-bicyclopentanes (BCPs), [2.2.1]-bicyclohexanes (BCHs), [3.1.1]-bicycloheptanes (BCHeps) and cubanes. Finally, we look to the future in relation to the preparation of other bioisoster units derived from complex 3D frameworks that are currently in high demand within both the pharmaceutical and materials science industries.
explanation: Contains precise, domain-specific language and a conventional review structure, which can be produced by humans but also by advanced AI. A minor grammar slip ('metabolism stability' instead of 'metabolic stability') reduces certainty.
